raw_transcript.speaker: Anna
raw_transcript.text: Guten Morgen! Was darf es sein?

raw_transcript.speaker: Robert
raw_transcript.text: Guten Morgen! Ich möchte zwei Brötchen und ein Stück Kuchen, bitte.

raw_transcript.speaker: Anna
raw_transcript.text: Natürlich! Welche Brötchen möchten Sie - die normalen oder die Vollkornbrötchen?

raw_transcript.speaker: Robert
raw_transcript.text: Die Vollkornbrötchen, bitte. Was kosten das?

raw_transcript.speaker: Anna
raw_transcript.text: Die Vollkornbrötchen kosten 80 Cent pro Stück, und der Kuchen kostet zwei Euro fünfzig. Das macht insgesamt vier Euro zehn.

raw_transcript.speaker: Robert
raw_transcript.text: Hier ist fünf Euro. Haben Sie auch Kaffee?

raw_transcript.speaker: Anna
raw_transcript.text: Ja, wir haben Kaffee - einen Euro achtzig für einen kleinen, zwei Euro zwanzig für einen großen. Möchten Sie einen dazu?

raw_transcript.speaker: Robert
raw_transcript.text: Ja, einen großen Kaffee bitte. Danke schön!

raw_transcript.speaker: Anna
raw_transcript.text: Gerne! Das macht dann sechs Euro dreißig insgesamt. Ihr Wechselgeld ist siebzig Cent. Guten Appetit!

reviewer_output.overall_summary: Robert handled a realistic bakery transaction confidently, using polite register and appropriate vocabulary throughout. The conversation flowed naturally and he successfully ordered multiple items, asked about prices, and managed a payment exchange. One grammatical error with interrogative word order slightly marred an otherwise strong performance.

errors.explanation: In a question with 'was' as the subject referring to a singular concept (the total price or the item group being discussed), the verb should be 'kostet' (3rd person singular); additionally 'das' here is singular, so the verb must agree accordingly.
errors.context: Die Vollkornbrötchen, bitte. Was kosten das?

vocabulary_highlights.note: introduced by Anna; good passive exposure
introduced by Anna; useful word to actively learn
key retail phrase; Robert heard it modelled twice — needs practice producing it

vocabulary_highlights.english: one (of those) in addition / on top
vocabulary_highlights.note: natural colloquial addition phrase used by Anna; good to absorb
used correctly by Anna; Robert should be able to produce this

strengths: Correctly used 'ich möchte' for polite requests rather than the more abrupt 'ich will'
Proper use of accusative article 'einen großen Kaffee' showing solid case awareness
Natural conversational flow with appropriate 'bitte' and 'Danke schön' at the right moments
Successfully navigated a multi-item order and a follow-up question about an additional product
Maintained formal 'Sie' register consistently, matching the bakery service context
reviewer_output.next_focus: Practice verb conjugation in direct questions: drill the pattern 'Was kostet das?' vs. 'Was kosten die Brötchen?' to solidify subject-verb agreement when asking about prices, and expand to similar shopping question structures (e.g., 'Wie viel kostet ...?', 'Was macht das?').
